chunks.text: RISC-V (Five) is Alive! - EECS at Berkeley RISC-V, an open-source instruction set architecture created at UC Berkeley is featured in an electronic design article titled “ RICS-V (Five) is Alive! ” RISC (Reduced Instruction Set Computer) was originally designed in 1982 by students with the direction of Professors David Patterson and Carlo Sequin . Since then, iterations of RISC have been developed. In 2010 Prof. Krste Asanovic , with the help of Prof. Patterson, decided to develop another version of RISC to help both academic and industrial users and RISC-V was published. Categories Faculty Research View Open Faculty Positions
